segments.text: I will extol you, O Lord, for you have drawn me up and have not let my foes rejoice over me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: O Lord my God, I cried to you for help, and you have healed me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: O Lord, you have brought up my soul from Sheol; you restored me to life from among those who go down to the pit.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Sing praises to the Lord, O you his saints, and give thanks to his holy name.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For his anger is but for a moment, and his favor is for a lifetime. Weeping may tarry for the night, but joy comes with the morning.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: there is but a moment in his anger

segments.text: As for me, I said in my prosperity, 'I shall never be moved.'
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: By your favor, O Lord, you made my mountain stand strong; you hid your face; I was dismayed.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: To you, O Lord, I cry, and to the Lord I plead for mercy:
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: 'What profit is there in my death, if I go down to the pit? Will the dust praise you? Will it tell of your faithfulness?'
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Hear, O Lord, and be merciful to me! O Lord, be my helper!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: You have turned for me my mourning into dancing; you have loosed my sackcloth and clothed me with gladness,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: so that my glory may sing your praise and not be silent. O Lord my God, I will give thanks to you forever!
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: that is, my tongue, or, my soul
